 -Rules : Agent(?a), hasCapability(?a, ?c), PushingCapability(?c), Object(?o), hasDisposition(?o, ?d), PushableDisposition(?d), isApproachableBy(?o,?a), EndEffector(?g), hasComponent(?a,?g), hasApplicableForce(?g,?w1), requiresForce(?o,?w2), equal(?w2,?w1) -> canPush(?a, ?o)
	PushingCapability|EquivalentTo|(isCapabilityOf some (hasGripper min 1 Gripper)
	PushableDisposition|EquivalentTo|(isDispositionOf some (hasPart some RollablePart))
answers.answer: the robot can push the mini shopping cart because it has a pushing capability, which is equivalent to having a mechanical hand as an end effector, and the mini shopping cart is pushable due to its caster part being rollable, with both requiring forces of 31 units.
